prompts.description: Add checkedItems to Zustand store with persist and actions
prompts.prompt: In lib/meal-store.ts, add a new store slice for grocery checklist persistence. Add `checkedItems: Record<string, boolean>` to the store state (default: {}). Add two actions: `toggleCheckedItem(ingredientName: string)` that flips the boolean for that key, and `clearCheckedItems()` that resets the entire object to {}. Also add `resetGroceryOnRegen: boolean` (default: false) to the `appPrefs` slice with a `setResetGroceryOnRegen(value: boolean)` action. Include `checkedItems` and `resetGroceryOnRegen` in the persist partialize so they survive page reload. Follow the existing PROFILE_VERSION and migrate() pattern — if a stored profile lacks these keys, normalizeUserProfile() should default them. Do NOT change any existing store slices, actions, or the weekPlan shape. Verify: npm run build passes with no TypeScript errors.

prompts.description: Replace local checkedItems useState in grocery-list.tsx with Zustand
prompts.prompt: Building on prompt #1. In components/grocery-list.tsx, remove the local `useState` that tracks checked items (look for a state variable holding a Set or Record of checked ingredient names). Replace all reads and writes to that local state with `checkedItems`, `toggleCheckedItem()`, and `clearCheckedItems()` from the Zustand store (lib/meal-store.ts). The checkbox onChange handler should call `toggleCheckedItem(ingredientName)`. The checked prop on each checkbox should read `checkedItems[ingredientName] ?? false`. Do NOT change the grocery list grouping logic, UI layout, or any other state. Do NOT auto-clear on mount. Verify: npm run build passes and grocery-list renders without errors.

prompts.description: Add reset-on-regen toggle to Settings and wire clearCheckedItems to generateMealPlan
prompts.prompt: Building on prompts #1 and #2. Two changes: (A) In components/settings-screen.tsx, add a toggle row labeled 'Reset grocery checklist when plan regenerates' using an existing shadcn Switch component. Wire it to `resetGroceryOnRegen` and `setResetGroceryOnRegen()` from Zustand. Place it in the same section as other app preference toggles (dark mode, language, etc.). (B) In lib/meal-store.ts, inside the `generateMealPlan()` action, immediately after a successful new weekPlan is set, check `get().appPrefs.resetGroceryOnRegen` — if true, call `clearCheckedItems()`. If false, do nothing (preserve checks). Do NOT change any other regeneration logic, error handling, or loading state. Verify: npm run build passes.

prompts.description: Add Diet Type and Meals Per Day fields to Edit Profile modal in daily-view.tsx
prompts.prompt: In components/daily-view.tsx, locate the Edit Profile modal (search for 'Edit Profile' or 'editProfile'). Add two new fields inside the modal form, after the existing fields: (1) Diet Type — a Select with four options matching the existing DietType enum in lib/meal-store.ts: 'balanced', 'low-carb', 'high-protein', 'keto'. Display labels: 'Balanced', 'Low Carb', 'High Protein', 'Keto'. (2) Meals Per Day — a number Input, integer only, min 2 max 5. Both fields must read initial values from `userProfile.dietType` and `userProfile.mealsPerDay` (or `mealPlanConfig.mealsPerDay` — check which field the store uses). On save, call `setUserProfile()` with updated values, then immediately call `calculateNutritionTargets()` from lib/nutrition.ts to recalculate macro targets. Do NOT trigger meal plan regeneration on save. Do NOT remove or reorder the existing 7 fields. Use existing shadcn Select and Input components from components/ui/. Verify: npm run build passes with no TypeScript errors.

prompts.description: Verify Edit Profile save updates macro targets displayed in Daily View
prompts.prompt: Building on prompt #4. In components/daily-view.tsx, confirm that after the Edit Profile modal saves, the macro target display in Daily View (calories, protein, carbs, fat targets shown per day) reflects the updated values from the recalculated userProfile. If the macro display reads directly from `userProfile` store state, this should already work — verify and add no unnecessary re-fetch logic. If the macro display uses local component state that is set only on mount, update it to derive from Zustand store state reactively (useStore selector). Do NOT change the visual layout or existing fields of the Edit Profile modal. Do NOT change any API calls or the meal plan data itself. Verify: npm run build passes and no TypeScript errors.

prompts.description: Wrap regenerate action in Settings with shadcn AlertDialog confirmation
prompts.prompt: In components/settings-screen.tsx, locate the function or button that triggers meal plan regeneration (search for 'generateMealPlan', 'handleRegenerate', or 'Regenerate'). Wrap this trigger with a shadcn AlertDialog. Import AlertDialog components from components/ui/alert-dialog — if that file does not exist, run `npx shadcn@latest add alert-dialog` first. The AlertDialog must have: Title: 'Regenerate your plan?' Body: 'Your current week will be replaced with a new AI-generated plan. This cannot be undone.' Two buttons: 'Cancel' (variant default, closes dialog, no regeneration) and 'Regenerate' (variant destructive, closes dialog, proceeds with existing regeneration logic). The existing regeneration logic must not change — only wrap the trigger. Do NOT add AlertDialog to any other action. Verify: npm run build passes.

prompts.description: Full integration type-check and build verification
prompts.prompt: Run a full TypeScript type-check and build verification across all files modified in this feature branch. Check for: (1) any implicit `any` types introduced in lib/meal-store.ts, components/grocery-list.tsx, components/daily-view.tsx, or components/settings-screen.tsx; (2) missing or incorrect imports; (3) Zustand store shape mismatches between what actions write and what components read. Fix any TypeScript errors found. Do NOT change business logic — only fix type errors and import issues. After all fixes, verify: npm run build passes with zero errors and zero warnings related to the changed files.
